summary: The reproduction bundle clones the pinned vulnerable repository state, imports the vulnerable multimodal prompt code, and verifies that a file://localhost/etc/passwd#.jpg payload leaks the exact /etc/passwd bytes through the generated data URL.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the multimodal prompt path to treat file://localhost/etc/passwd#.jpg as an image, open /etc/passwd, and embed the exact file bytes into the generated data:image/jpeg;base64 payload.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the target repository at commit 0f412de0a4694680684b0981d08faae38152f80b, built the Docker image, and printed EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS after decoding a data URL whose bytes matched /etc/passwd exactly. The run also printed the first leaked line: root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash.
verification_method: The helper script decodes the generated data URL and compares the bytes byte-for-byte with /etc/passwd from inside the container. The run succeeds only if the leaked bytes match exactly and the script prints EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS.
evidence_produced_by_run: The helper decoded the generated data:image/jpeg;base64 payload and verified it matched /etc/passwd byte-for-byte.
The run printed the first leaked line from /etc/passwd: root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
